example_title: PackCompleteness happy path: Complete PDRA-01 VLOS pack (Scotland pattern)
given: Edinburgh Napier University submits PDRA-01 VLOS pack: ConOps v2.2, Personnel & Training Manual, Safety Case, Risk Register, Aircraft Technical Data, Insurance (GBP 10M+), Ground Survey, Environmental Assessment. All 8 artefacts present and validly formatted.
then: All 8 artefact types flagged GREEN; case transitions to INTAKE_COMPLETE within 10 minutes
and: Case gates to Phase 1 SORA Assessment

example_title: PackCompleteness error: Sparse BVLOS Swarm pack missing SORA Swarm-Aware section
given: CTRL-ALT-DEFEAT submits BVLOS Swarm trial (5x RigiTech Eiger, Spadeadam): SORA lacks swarm-specific sections, ML Model Manifest absent
when: Artefact Detector detects 7 of 8 mandatory types
then: SORA flagged AMBER; ML Model Manifest flagged RED; case status = RETURN_FOR_CLARIFICATION
and: Coaching feedback sent on swarm-aware SORA requirements
notes: Boundary case: swarm-specific requirements drive higher completeness bar

example_title: Swarm-Aware SORA SAIL mismatch: 5-aircraft autonomous swarm declared SAIL-I, actual SAIL-IV required
given: Spadeadam swarm trial: 5x Eiger, autonomous swarm, GNSS/C2 denial, declared SAIL-I. But SORA lacks multi-agent hazard analysis
when: SORA assessor applies JARUS SORA v2.5 Appendix C (Swarm)
then: Assessor determines actual SAIL-IV required due to swarm interdependencies
and: Case returns for re-assessment; applicant must accept SAIL-IV rigor or narrow scope
notes: Swarm operations require higher assurance levels than single-aircraft

example_title: Geofence breach action: 55.3m breach detected, RTL within 500ms
given: Spadeadam: 5x Eiger at 54.9167N 2.5667W, 50m geofence tolerance; wind gust causes 55.3m breach
then: RTL command issued within 100ms; landing completed within 3.2s total
and: Audit log records GEOFENCE_BREACH and RTL_COMPLETE
notes: Tight tolerance, fast detection, safe response

example_title: DAA non-cooperative encounter: Category A intruder, autonomous climb executed
given: Spadeadam trial: 5x aircraft at 500ft; Cessna 172 intruder at 2000ft; DAA detects at 2.8nm
when: DAA resolver activates; autonomous climb command issued
then: Swarm climbs to 1100ft; intruder passes below with 900ft separation
notes: DO-365 DAA performance: sufficient detection range and time budget

example_title: C2 loss during swarm consensus: AC4 consensus timeout, autonomous RTL triggered
given: Spadeadam intentional C2 denial test: 5x Eiger in AC4 consensus mode; C2 powered down at t=120s
when: C2 loss detected; aircraft enter autonomous mode; voting window opens
then: Consensus resolves on MAINTAIN_ALTITUDE; timeout at t=420s triggers RTL; landing at t=425.8s
and: Audit trail shows CONSENSUS_RESOLVED and LANDING_COMPLETE
notes: Swarm survives C2 loss for 300s via distributed consensus and timeout-driven RTL

example_title: Meaningful Human Control override latency: Crew presses button, swarm halts within 50ms
given: Spadeadam autonomous formation hold: crew observes altitude deviation on GCS, presses emergency override button at t=45.2s
when: Button press triggers C2 uplink; override command received by all aircraft at t=50ms
then: All 5 aircraft transition to hover; crew regains full manual control
and: Situational awareness maintained; 10Hz telemetry; workload = 1 concurrent alarm (within limit)
notes: Crew preserves situational awareness and authority over swarm

example_title: Independent Flight Termination: RF kill-switch cuts motors within 350ms
given: Spadeadam dual-link failure at t=623s: swarm drifting uncontrolled; Range Safety Officer activates independent termination RF kill-switch
when: Kill-code transmitted at 915 MHz independent link; received by all aircraft at t=50ms
then: All motors unpowered within 350ms; aircraft descend and land safely
and: Post-incident inspection confirms RF kill-switch worked; no secondary hazard
notes: Ultimate safety net: RSO can force safe descent when all other systems fail

example_title: CAA/MAA Apportionment: Spadeadam trial signed matrix, civil panel vs military range
given: CTRL-ALT-DEFEAT applies for BVLOS Swarm at Spadeadam (MOD-owned). Apportionment matrix maps 15 approval gates: CAA owns 11, MAA owns 1, joint gates 3
then: CAA Lead Inspector and MAA Air Safety Officer review and sign; apportionment becomes binding contract
and: Case status = APPORTIONMENT_COMPLETE; case progresses to Phase 1 SORA Assessment
notes: Apportionment prevents disputes over regulatory authority boundaries

example_title: Classification custodian: SECRET evidence triggers CAP 722H / JSP 440 security path
given: Hypothetical MOD trial: applicant submits SECRET sensor specifications (CLASSIFIED optical payload). Envelope marked OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE but document lacks SECRET header
when: Artefact Detector scans classification metadata; confidence 85% that document is SECRET
then: Case escalates to Classification Review Officer (CRO); document segregated to secure-access pool
and: SORA proceeds on OFFICIAL docs; SECRET docs reviewed by cleared subset only; decision record redacted

example_data.mitigation: Redacted summary vs full SECRET doc

addresses_finding: Implicit Scope Risk GOAL-009; REQ018 undefined
notes: MOD classified trials require special handling; secure pathways protect disclosure

example_title: Panel-action audit log write: Lead Inspector overrides AI flag; latency <500ms
given: SORA Assessment: AI tool flags ConOps missing 3rd loss-of-separation mitigation item. Lead Inspector John Smith overrides, noting swarm-consensus-timeout is documented in Section 4.3 Table 4-1
when: John submits override with rationale on panel interface
then: Audit-log entry recorded: OVERRIDE_SUBMITTED at 2026-04-23 14:15:32.847 with 342ms write latency
and: Override is immutable; case history shows John's name, timestamp, reasoning permanently
notes: Audit trail maintains accountability while preserving human judgment authority

example_title: Readiness Check fails: Mandatory Duty Holder signature missing on Safety Case
given: Applicant resubmits pack after RETURN_FOR_CLARIFICATION. All content now satisfactory. But Safety Case Section 1.1 Duty Holder Declaration signature line is blank (only name printed)
when: Thorough Gate reviewer checks signatures section-by-section
then: Case transitions to RETURN_FOR_CLARIFICATION; applicant asked to obtain Duty Holder signature
and: Applicant resubmits within 3 days with hand signature; case transitions to INTAKE_COMPLETE
notes: Signature is blocking gate; ensures organizational accountability for trial authorization

example_title: Decision Record for approval-with-conditions: 5 conditions + 3 review triggers, Phase 1 only
given: Spadeadam trial passes all technical gates. Panel convenes: CAA Lead Inspector, Autonomy Specialist, MAA Air Safety. Panel decides APPROVE WITH CONDITIONS for Phase 1 only
when: Decision Record document structured per CAP 722D Appendix E: Summary, Regulatory Basis, Approval Conditions, Review Triggers
then: Decision Record issued: Phase 1 Scope Limit (VLOS 500m, 2-aircraft max, daylight only), CAA Observer Required, C2 Link Continuity, Emergency Authority, Incident Reporting
and: Review Triggers: Phase 2 Readiness Gate (after 10 successful flights), Incident-Triggered Re-assessment, Workload Limit Breach
notes: Conditional approval balances flexibility with regulatory authority; conditions binding

example_title: Decision Record for refusal: Structured document enumerates 3 blockers with clause citations
given: Hypothetical applicant applies for BVLOS Swarm: 10x DJI Matrice, autonomous swarm, ML collision avoidance, NO crew override. Panel identifies crew control violation
when: Decision Record issued citing REQ010, CAP 722D Section 3.1
then: REFUSE AUTHORIZATION with 3 blocking findings: (1) Meaningful Human Control violation (crew monitor-only), (2) ML Explainability insufficient (no off-distribution evidence), (3) Emergent Behavior Bounds undefined
and: Path to reapplication specified: redesign ConOps, provide ML robustness evidence, submit emergent-behavior assurance case. 30-day appeal window. Expected re-review 8-12 weeks
notes: Refusal is not dead-end; provides clear feedback and reapplication path. Crew override authority non-negotiable

example_title: Post-decision archive: Decision PDF + evidence bundle + deliberation log + clause-library version pin
given: Spadeadam trial Phase 1 + Phase 2 complete. All Phase 1 flights successful (no MOR). Phase 2 approved. Trial closed 2026-06-15. Archival process begins
when: CMS triggers post-decision archival workflow: collect documents, pin clause-library version, extract deliberation log, generate archive summary
then: Archival package created: 280 MB (Decision PDFs, Evidence Bundles, Deliberation Log, Clause Library v2.1 pin, Archive Summary)
and: Archive stored in CAA Secure Archive (Google Cloud Storage, geo-redundant, 10-year retention, access-logged)
notes: Archive preserves complete audit trail for post-incident AAIB investigation and regulatory audit
